questions.text: The heart lies within which thoracic cavity compartment?
Which portion of the heart forms the base?

answers.text: Left atrium and small amount of right atrium

questions.text: What is the primary function of the fibrous pericardium?

answers.text: To produce serous fluid for lubrication
To anchor the heart in the mediastinum and prevent overstretching
To supply blood to the myocardium
To form the inner lining of the heart chambers

questions.text: Which layer of the heart wall contains the main coronary arteries on its surface?
Cardiac muscle tissue is described as striated and involuntary. Which characteristic allows it to contract as a coordinated unit?
The right atrium receives deoxygenated blood from all of the following EXCEPT:
The left ventricle is the thickest chamber because it must:

answers.text: Overcome the high pressure of the systemic circulation
Receive blood from the entire body
Generate the electrical impulse for the heart

questions.text: The fossa ovalis in the interatrial septum marks the location of the:
What is the primary function of the fibrous skeleton of the heart?

answers.text: To provide structural support and prevent premature electrical conduction

questions.text: The coronary sulcus separates which structures?
Which valve prevents backflow of blood from the left ventricle into the left atrium?
During ventricular systole, the papillary muscles contract to:

answers.text: Pull the chordae tendineae and prevent AV valve prolapse
Push blood through the coronary arteries

questions.text: The semilunar valves are anchored by and consist of:

answers.text: Fibrous skeleton; three crescent moon-shaped cusps

questions.text: Pulmonary circulation is described as a low-pressure system because it:

answers.text: Involves shorter distances and less resistance than systemic circulation
Involves only the left side of the heart

questions.text: Which coronary artery branch supplies oxygenated blood to the walls of both ventricles?
The great cardiac vein drains into which structure?
The small cardiac vein drains the area of the heart supplied by which artery?

answers.text: Right coronary artery and marginal branch

questions.text: Which artery supplies oxygenated blood to the brain?
The dorsalis pedis pulse is located where?
The largest vein in the body, returning blood from the lower body, is the:
During phase 0 of the cardiac action potential, rapid depolarization is caused by:

answers.text: Calcium influx through slow Ca2+ channels
Sodium influx through voltage-gated fast Na+ channels

questions.text: Which phase of the action potential is known as the plateau phase and sustains cardiac contraction?
During the absolute refractory period, the cardiac cell:

answers.text: Can be stimulated by a stronger than normal stimulus
Will not respond to any further stimulation

questions.text: Which component of the cardiac conduction system has an intrinsic rate of 40-60 bpm?
The Purkinje fibers rapidly conduct the action potential from the apex upward to ensure:

answers.text: The atria contract before the ventricles
Ventricular contraction begins at the apex and moves upward
The AV node delays the impulse

questions.text: The P wave on an ECG corresponds to which electrical event?
Isovolumetric contraction occurs when:

answers.text: The AV valves are open and SL valves are closed
Both the AV and SL valves are closed
The AV valves are closed and SL valves are open
Both the AV and SL valves are open

questions.text: End-diastolic volume (EDV) in a typical resting adult is approximately:
A patient has a heart rate of 80 bpm and a stroke volume of 65 mL. What is their cardiac output?
Which of the following would decrease stroke volume?
The tunica media of a blood vessel is primarily responsible for:

answers.text: Regulating lumen diameter through vasoconstriction and vasodilation
Anchoring the vessel to surrounding tissues

questions.text: Elastic arteries are characterized by:

answers.text: A tunica media dominated by smooth muscle
A tunica media dominated by elastic fibers
A thick tunica externa relative to their diameter
Greater capacity for vasoconstriction than other arteries

questions.text: Capillaries are known as exchange vessels because they:

answers.text: Have thick muscular walls to pump blood
Lack a tunica media and externa, maximizing surface area for diffusion
Are innervated by the autonomic nervous system

questions.text: Precapillary sphincters function to:

answers.text: Prevent blood from entering the heart
Regulate blood flow into specific capillary beds
Close the semilunar valves during diastole
Stimulate the SA node during exercise

questions.text: Approximately what percentage of blood volume is contained in systemic veins and venules at rest?
The vasa vasorum supplies:

answers.text: Oxygen and nutrients to the outer wall of blood vessels
Nerve fibers to the SA node

questions.text: Which mechanism of capillary exchange uses pinocyte vesicles to move large lipid-insoluble molecules across the endothelium?
In Starling's law of the capillaries, which pressure promotes filtration of fluid out of the capillary?
A decrease in blood albumin would most likely cause:

answers.text: Decreased BCOP and increased filtration leading to edema

questions.text: Vascular resistance is increased by all of the following EXCEPT:
The respiratory pump assists venous return by:

answers.text: Compressing abdominal veins and decompressing thoracic veins during inhalation
Opening the aortic valve during exhalation

questions.text: The cardiovascular center is located in the:
When blood pressure rises, baroreceptors respond by:

answers.text: Sending slower impulses to increase sympathetic tone
Sending faster impulses to increase parasympathetic stimulation and decrease sympathetic stimulation

questions.text: Chemoreceptors detect all of the following EXCEPT:
Antidiuretic hormone (ADH) increases blood pressure by:

answers.text: Causing vasoconstriction and helping the kidneys retain water
Blocking calcium channels in the heart

questions.text: Autoregulation allows a tissue to adjust its blood flow based on:

answers.text: Hormonal signals from the adrenal glands

questions.text: A patient with a myocardial infarction has difficulty maintaining adequate cardiac output. This is most consistent with which type of shock?
A pulmonary embolism lodged in the pulmonary arteries causes which type of shock?
The body's initial homeostatic response to shock includes all of the following EXCEPT:
Myogenic response refers to:

answers.text: The heart's ability to generate its own action potential
Vessels contracting more forcefully when stretched and relaxing when stretching less
The release of nitric oxide by endothelial cells
The effect of epinephrine on heart rate
